config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar utama, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kepentingan masyarakat bisa berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan dasar, martabat, dan keadilan bagi tiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati dan kepentingan umum dijalankan secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify abuse of minorities, dissenters, and unpopular people. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute: public safety, public health, and basic order sometimes require limits, but those limits need to be narrow, transparent, and accountable. The key question is not whether society matters, but whether the sacrifice of individual rights is truly necessary and whether the burden is being spread fairly. If a policy cannot pass that test, then it is usually society demanding too much from the individual.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman kita di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman kritik, tekanan pada kelompok kecil, atau kebijakan yang tidak adil bagi orang biasa. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar supaya tidak berubah menjadi alat kekuasaan yang sewenang-wenang. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, tetapi titik berangkatnya tetap perlindungan hak individu, bukan pengorbanan individu demi masyarakat secara otomatis.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society’s interests the default priority over individual rights, because in the United States that framing has too often been a cover for coercion and abuse. I do not deny that public safety, health, and order sometimes justify limits, but those limits should be exceptional, narrowly tailored, and open to challenge; otherwise “the common good” becomes a blank check for the powerful. What matters most to me is that the burden is justified, necessary, and shared fairly, not dumped on minorities, dissidents, or people with less power. A society that routinely sacrifices individual rights in the name of society is, in my view, not protecting society well at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan warga yang kritis atau kelompok kecil. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasan hak harus benar-benar perlu, jelas dasarnya, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk bertindak sewenang-wenang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak dasar sambil mengejar kepentingan bersama secara adil. Jadi, prioritas awal tetap pada hak individu, bukan pada kepentingan masyarakat secara otomatis.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in the United States we have too much history of “society first” being used to excuse discrimination, censorship, and abuse of unpopular people. At the same time, I am not saying individual rights are unlimited; public health, safety, and basic order can justify real restrictions, but only when they are necessary, narrow, and accountable. What I reject is the idea that society gets automatic priority, because that turns “the common good” into a vague excuse for power. To me, a legitimate society protects individual rights first and only limits them when there is a clear, fair, and proportionate reason.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
